     The Numbers Business Report

Long Shot (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $30,316,271Details
International Box Office $23,308,153
Worldwide Box Office $53,624,424
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,922,131
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,230,452
Total $3,152,583
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Fred Flarsky is a gifted and free-spirited journalist with an affinity for trouble. Charlotte Field is one of the most influential women in the world. Smart, sophisticated, and accomplished, she’s a powerhouse diplomat with a talent for… well, mostly everything. The two have nothing in common, except that she was his babysitter and childhood crush. When Fred unexpectedly reconnects with Charlotte, he charms her with his self-deprecating humor and his memories of her youthful idealism. As she prepares to make a run for the Presidency, Charlotte impulsively hires Fred as her speechwriter, much to the dismay of her trusted advisors. A fish out of water on Charlotte’s elite team, Fred is unprepared for her glamorous lifestyle in the limelight. However, sparks fly as their unmistakable chemistry leads to a round-the-world romance and a series of unexpected and dangerous incidents.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$9,740,064 (32.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.11 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:56.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,230 opening theaters/3,230 max. theaters, 3.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $39,583,259

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 3rd, 2019 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: May 1st, 2019 (Wide) (Singapore)
May 2nd, 2019 (Wide) (Suriname)
May 2nd, 2019 (Wide) (Slovenia)
May 2nd, 2019 (Wide), released as Seduz-me Se És Capaz (Portugal)
May 2nd, 2019 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
Video Release: July 16th, 2019 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong sexual content, language throughout and some drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2573 (Cert #52075), 4/10/2019)
Running Time: 125 minutes
Plot point: Childhood Friends, Investigative Journalist, Politicians, Romance
Social setting: Political Campaign
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Point Grey, Denver & Delilah Films, Good Universe, Lionsgate
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Fred Flarsky is a gifted and free-spirited journalist with an affinity for trouble. Charlotte Field is one of the most influential women in the world. Smart, sophisticated, and accomplished, she’s a powerhouse diplomat with a talent for… well, mostly everything. The two have nothing in common, except that she was his babysitter and childhood crush. When Fred unexpectedly reconnects with Charlotte, he charms her with his self-deprecating humor and his memories of her youthful idealism. As she prepares to make a run for the Presidency, Charlotte impulsively hires Fred as her speechwriter, much to the dismay of her trusted advisors. A fish out of water on Charlotte’s elite team, Fred is unprepared for her glamorous lifestyle in the limelight. However, sparks fly as their unmistakable chemistry leads to a round-the-world romance and a series of unexpected and dangerous incidents.
